useCases.Name: Procurement #1 - Historical Spend Analysis & Price Insights
useCases.Headline: Real-time spend cube (category × vendor × region × time) on 3-year SAP data with BPV-gap and anomaly detection; replaces 3–5 days of quarterly Excel work and gives leadership self-serve analytics
useCases.sfsLabel: Procurement #1 - Historical Spend Analysis & Price Insights
useCases.Readiness Caveat: SAP purchase base is strong; taxonomy, BPV and anomaly rules need procurement workshops.

useCases.Name: Procurement #2 - Real-Time Import Data Analysis
useCases.Headline: Real-time EXIM/customs intelligence dashboard benchmarking SFS purchase prices vs. CIF/landed cost; tracks supplier share, origin pricing dynamics, and pushes weekly alerts on import benchmark deviations and large competitor trades
useCases.Readiness Caveat: EXIM provider, HSN mapping, landed-cost normalization and provider lag must be validated.

useCases.Name: Procurement #3 - AI-Driven Preparation Material for Negotiations
useCases.Headline: LLM-orchestrated negotiation brief generator combining SAP purchase history, EXIM benchmarks, commodity indices, and seasonal patterns; cuts prep time from 2–3 days to <30 minutes and standardizes negotiation quality across buyers
useCases.sfsLabel: Procurement #3 - AI-Driven Preparation Material for Negotiations
useCases.Readiness Caveat: Moved out of normal Tier 2 because it depends on EXIM/commodity feeds and negotiation outcome capture.

useCases.Name: Procurement #5 - Vendor Creditworthiness & Fraud Check
useCases.Headline: Automated vendor risk scoring at onboarding and ongoing monitoring via GST portal, MCA21, credit bureau, and court records; composite tier (green/amber/red) gates Ariba onboarding and triggers pre-PO alerts; protects the 300+ vendor long tail from default, fraud, and GST input-tax-credit risk
useCases.Readiness Caveat: Mostly external risk/compliance data plus entity resolution and legal/privacy approval.

useCases.Name: Procurement #4 - AI-Led Vendor Identification & Recommendation
useCases.Headline: AI-ranked vendor recommendations per commodity combining SAP performance history (GRN + price), EXIM-sourced new vendor signals, and UC #5 risk filters; one-click Ariba RFQ trigger; reduces vendor discovery time >90% and surfaces direct-manufacturer alternatives to traders
useCases.Readiness Caveat: Keep in Tier 2 because the owned internal base can start; external signals improve the recommendation layer.

useCases.Name: Procurement #6 - AI-Driven Should-Cost Analysis (across the top 5-10 products)
useCases.Headline: Automated cost build-up calculator per SKU combining raw material indices, labor rates, energy/utility costs, freight, and overhead benchmarks; outputs target cost band that auto-updates as inputs change; arms sourcing managers with an independent reference to challenge vendor quotes systematically
useCases.sfsLabel: Procurement #6 - AI-Driven Should-Cost Analysis (across the top 5-10 products)
useCases.Readiness Caveat: Moved out of normal Tier 2 because it needs external market feeds and category-specific assumptions.

useCases.Name: Field Force #1 - Field Performance Tracking Dashboard
useCases.Headline: Unified real-time sales performance dashboard across SAP, Growthbook, and Saathi with drill-down from Sales Head → RBM → TBM → dealer → SKU; mid-month early-warning alerts on target attainment; replaces 4–6 hours/week of manual report consolidation per field manager
useCases.sfsLabel: Field Force #1 - Field Performance Tracking Dashboard
useCases.Readiness Caveat: Fast descriptive base; liquidation and secondary-sales completeness require new SFS capture.

useCases.Name: Field Force #4 - Dealer Profiles & Field-Visit Conversation Inputs (Saathi app)
useCases.Headline: Consolidated dealer 360° profile inside Saathi App combining SAP transactions, Saathi engagement, scheme participation, and AI-captured TBM voice notes; auto-tiered Platinum/Gold/Silver/Bronze; eliminates 2–4 weeks of context-rebuild when TBMs change territories and gives HO leadership systematic dealer mix analytics
useCases.sfsLabel: Field Force #4 - Dealer Profiles & Field-Visit Conversation Inputs (Saathi app)
useCases.Readiness Caveat: Strong dealer/SAP base; relationship intelligence and voice capture need workflow validation.

useCases.Name: Field Force #2 - Activity Analytics (Growthbook + SAP)
useCases.Headline: Automated field force activity capture via Saathi App (in-app voice + check-in) combined with sales data; surfaces visit cadence, dealer coverage %, time allocation, and call types; correlates activity intensity with sales outcomes; coverage-gap alerts and data-grounded RBM coaching inputs replace anecdotal management
useCases.Readiness Caveat: ODS has only a name and no detailed content; field map is inferred from sibling use cases and must be scoped with SFS.

useCases.Name: Field Force #3 - Pricing, Policy & Scheme Dissemination Chatbot
useCases.Headline: Multi-lingual conversational chatbot inside Saathi App answering scheme queries (eligibility, discount slabs, claim process) grounded in actual circulars via RAG; eliminates TBM scheme-rule errors that today cause claim rejections and dealer trust damage; reduces ~30% of TBM time spent on scheme Q&A
useCases.sfsLabel: Field Force #3 - Pricing, Policy & Scheme Dissemination Chatbot
useCases.Readiness Caveat: Circulars, pricing conditions and eligibility rules must be structured.

useCases.Name: Field Force #5 - AI-Enabled Sales Coaching
useCases.Headline: Personalized RBM coaching surface combining activity patterns, sales outcomes, and conversation analysis from actual dealer voice captures; specific behavior-grounded coaching recommendations replace generic anecdotal advice; feeds L&D personalized training paths based on cohort skill-gap patterns
useCases.Readiness Caveat: Approved coaching content, voice/call capture, classification and coaching rubric are required.

useCases.Name: Field Force #6 - Unified Product & Partner-Level Visibility
useCases.Headline: Consolidated cross-BU dealer × product dashboard surfacing product-mix breakdown, cross-sell opportunity scoring, and cannibalization signals across Seeds / CP / SPN; replaces per-BU siloed views with portfolio-level dealer concentration and launch impact analytics
useCases.sfsLabel: Field Force #6 - Unified Product & Partner-Level Visibility
useCases.Readiness Caveat: Strong SAP base; secondary-sales, liquidation and PoG-led intelligence need new capture.

useCases.Name: Field Force #7 - Real-Time Warehouse Stock Visibility
useCases.Headline: Real-time consolidated stock view across SFS-owned warehouses + vendor / external warehouses + in-transit stock inside Saathi App at order entry; surfaces multi-source availability with staleness indicators, expiry status, recommended source warehouse, and substitution suggestions; eliminates TBM credibility loss from over-committed delivery dates and gives Dispatch + Commercial early warning on low-stock and expiry risks
useCases.sfsLabel: Field Force #7 - Real-Time Warehouse Stock Visibility
useCases.Readiness Caveat: SAP stock is likely available; vendor warehouse, in-transit, substitution and freshness need validation.

useCases.Name: Field Force #8 - Early Warning for Sales Performance & Dealer Churn Risk
useCases.Headline: Monthly churn risk score per dealer combining sales decline, scheme participation drop, complaint signals from voice captures, payment behavior, and GST default flags; contributing-factor breakdown routes to TBM (action) + Central Finance (credit risk) + RBM (coaching context); enables targeted intervention before churn becomes irreversible
useCases.Original Analysis Name: 13. Early Warning for Sales Performance & Dealer Churn Risk
useCases.sfsLabel: Field Force #8 - Early Warning for Sales Performance & Dealer Churn Risk
useCases.originalAnalysisName: 13. Early Warning for Sales Performance & Dealer Churn Risk
useCases.Readiness Caveat: Churn definition, intervention tracking and risk labels needed. Field count is 19 after removing BPV Gap.

useCases.Name: Enterprise Assistant #1 - Enterprise Chat Assistant (Supply & Sales)
useCases.Headline: Natural-language chat surface for leadership over a curated enterprise metrics layer covering Supply + Sales; collapses monthly review prep from 3–5 days to half a day, time-to-answer on ad-hoc queries from days to minutes; eliminates KPI definition drift via single shared metrics dictionary; weekly 'what changed' digest pushes outliers proactively
useCases.sfsLabel: Enterprise Assistant #1 - Enterprise Chat Assistant (Supply & Sales)
useCases.Readiness Caveat: Strong Wave 1 if scoped to confirmed SAP BW / Power BI extracts and metric dictionary.

properties.Description: Weighted composite score across GST + MCA + credit + litigation + tenure components; single numeric indicator driving risk tier classification
properties.Notes / Open Questions:
Component weightings to be agreed with Risk team and Supply Chain Head. May need category-specific weights (e.g., CP/SPN with higher GST default sensitivity due to ITC implications).

NOTE — distinct from UC #4's Vendor Composite Performance Score: RISK SCORE answers 'is this vendor financially/legally safe to transact with' (GST + MCA + credit + litigation); PERFORMANCE SCORE answers 'should we recommend this vendor for this category' (price + delivery + quality + risk-tier penalty). CROSS-UC VALUE: directly feeds vendor recommendation ranking in UC #4 — 'don't recommend red-tier vendors' is a critical filter in the AI-Led Vendor Identification flow.

properties.Description: Methodology asset per commodity defining cost decomposition: raw material components → labor → energy/utility → manufacturing overhead → freight → vendor margin assumption. Foundational template that drives the should-cost calculator; component definitions and weights vary by commodity (seed treatment differs from agrochemical AI synthesis)
properties.Notes / Open Questions: FOUNDATIONAL ASSET for this UC. The vendor margin assumption is encoded as a parameter within this template (varies by category and possibly vendor scale). Templates likely need to be commodity-specific given materially different cost structures (e.g., seed coating vs. CP active synthesis vs. packaging fabrication). Recommend starting with top 5–10 commodity templates, expanding to top 20 in phase 2.

Related Use Cases.name: Procurement #6 - AI-Driven Should-Cost Analysis (across the top 5-10 products)

properties.What's Needed to Make Available: Phase 1 (Methodology): workshops with category managers + technical/manufacturing experts to define cost build-up framework per commodity — component decomposition, weighting logic, vendor margin assumption rules. Phase 2 (Build): encode templates in the should-cost engine; map each template to underlying data sources (commodity index for RM, labor benchmark for labor, etc.). Phase 3 (Governance): version control + update cadence for templates as cost structures evolve.

properties.Description: Raw voice + transcript artifact captured by TBMs in Saathi App after dealer visits; primary capture mode is Voice Note (free-form, unstructured) to preserve colloquial nuance, vernacular phrasing, and compositional intelligence that a Voice AI Agent's question-answer structure would flatten; Voice AI Agent surface available as optional secondary mode for specific structured workflows (e.g., new-TBM onboarding, scheme-objection capture). Raw audio is the primary persisted artifact; transcript is derived.
properties.Notes / Open Questions:
DESIGN DECISION: Raw voice note is the PRIMARY capture surface. Voice AI Agent is secondary, not primary — the colloquial nuance, code-switched Hindi-English-local-language, and compositional intelligence in raw speech is itself a strategic moat (vernacular agri-sales voice corpus). An agent's question-answer scaffolding flattens this.

ON-GROUND TESTING REQUIRED before full rollout: pilot in 2–3 regions (Hindi-heavy, South Indian, mixed code-switched) over 2 months to validate (a) transcription quality across actual TBM languages and dialects; (b) field noise tolerance (markets, dealer shops, motorbikes); (c) domain vocabulary accuracy (seed variety names, agrochemical molecules, scheme names) — expect 15–25% errors without a glossary layer; (d) TBM adoption and trust dynamics; (e) privacy comfort levels.

FEASIBILITY ASSESSMENT: technically buildable today — 6–8 weeks to functional MVP, 3–4 months to production quality. STT options: AI4Bharat IndicWhisper / IndicConformer (open source, Indian-language specialist), Sarvam AI, Krutrim, Whisper-large-v3, Azure / Google STT. Self-hosting likely cheaper at SFS scale than commercial API pricing ($5–15K/month at scale).

RETENTION: raw audio should be persisted indefinitely. STT model quality is improving rapidly (3 generations of Indic models from AI4Bharat in 24 months); re-transcription in 12–18 months will be materially better.

PLATFORM CAPABILITY: the in-app voice capture infrastructure is reusable across UC #11 (Sales Coaching), UC #12 (Product & Partner Visibility), UC #14 (Churn Early Warning). Design as a Saathi platform feature.

properties.Description: Correlation analysis between activity patterns (visit cadence, call type mix, time allocation) and sales outcomes (primary sales, secondary sales, scheme participation, dealer tier movement); identifies high-leverage activity patterns from top-performing TBMs
properties.Notes / Open Questions:
DESIGN TENSION FLAGGED: Done well, this surfaces best-practice patterns that propagate across the field force (high-performing TBMs visit Platinum dealers more frequently, spend more time on discovery calls, etc.). Done badly, this becomes surveillance theater where TBMs game metrics (more visits regardless of quality, fewer complaint calls because they 'look bad').

MITIGATION: never report activity metrics in isolation — always paired with outcome metrics. Correlation insights surfaced as coaching prompts to RBMs (not as scorecards to TBMs). Use anonymized cohort benchmarking rather than individual public ranking. Periodic review by L&D to ensure correlations reflect actual best practices rather than artifacts.

properties.Description: Per-answer chatbot confidence score (0–1); below threshold triggers escalation pattern ('I'm not certain about this — please contact your Commercial Team representative'). Conservative threshold critical because scheme errors have material financial impact (claim rejections, dealer trust damage).
properties.Notes / Open Questions:
CRITICAL RISK-MANAGEMENT FIELD. Conservative threshold + escalation behavior is non-negotiable for go-live. The chatbot should never confidently hallucinate scheme rules — hallucinated confidence is more dangerous than uncertainty.

Design principle: prefer 'I don't know, escalate' over wrong answers. Threshold calibration during pilot to balance escalation rate (too many escalations = chatbot useless) vs. error rate (too many errors = chatbot dangerous).

Related Use Cases.name: Field Force #3 - Pricing, Policy & Scheme Dissemination Chatbot

properties.Description: Raw scheme circular PDF as issued by HO (or regional authority); the legal source of truth for scheme terms. Metadata captured alongside: BU, issuing authority, scope (national / regional / BU-specific / tier-specific / SKU-specific), validity start/end, supersession (which prior circulars are replaced), version, issue date.
properties.Notes / Open Questions:
RELATIONSHIP TO SCHEME DEFINITION (UC #7): Scheme Circular Document is the raw legal artifact; Scheme Definition is the structured extract. The chatbot's RAG cites back to specific circular passages; structured rules drive eligibility and discount computation. Keep both fields — they serve different purposes.

DISCOVERY OPEN: where circulars live today (central SharePoint, BU folders, WhatsApp, individual PCs); ingestion approach (push by Commercial Team on publish vs. scheduled pull); format consistency across Seeds / CP / SPN; versioning practice (amendments vs. re-issuance).

SCOPE METADATA is critical for chatbot filtering — not all circulars apply to all dealers. Scope filtering is a hard requirement before chatbot can answer eligibility queries reliably.

Related Use Cases.name: Field Force #3 - Pricing, Policy & Scheme Dissemination Chatbot

properties.What's Needed to Make Available: Phase 1: identify central repository for all circulars (or build one if fragmented); establish ingestion process (push by Commercial Team on publish, with metadata required at upload). Phase 2: LLM extraction pipeline parses circulars into structured Scheme Definition records; metadata fields (scope, validity, supersession) captured manually or LLM-extracted with human validation. Phase 3: versioning + supersession workflow.

properties.Description: Per-TBM per-dimension score derived from LLM analysis of voice captures against the Conversation Quality Framework rubrics. Surfaces strengths (high scores) and gaps (low scores). Surfaced to RBM with cohort-anonymized peer comparison; raw scores not shown to TBM directly — RBM contextualizes.
properties.Notes / Open Questions:
Score accuracy depends on (a) framework rubric quality, (b) LLM rating reliability across Indian-language captures, (c) sufficient capture volume per TBM for statistical stability. Recommend showing only rolling N-capture averages, not individual capture scores, to dampen noise.

PRIVACY: scores are RBM-visible; TBMs see only their own dimension-level trends (no peer comparison); HO sees only anonymized cohort distributions.

Related Use Cases.name: Field Force #5 - AI-Enabled Sales Coaching

properties.Description: LLM-generated specific coaching prompts grounded in (a) actual voice excerpts from the TBM's recent captures, (b) activity patterns, (c) peer benchmark gaps. Format: 'In your last 12 Platinum visits, you spent <5 min on cross-sell. Top-decile peers spend 12+ min. Here's an excerpt from your visit on [date]: [quote]. Consider asking: [example questions].' Routed to RBM, not directly to TBM.
properties.Notes / Open Questions:
DESIGN PRINCIPLE: AI surfaces patterns + suggests prompts; RBM delivers feedback to TBM as a human coach. Avoids 'TBMs being graded by a machine' failure mode.

Grounding in actual voice excerpts is critical — generic coaching advice is what already exists. Specificity is what makes this useful.

Frequency: recommend weekly batch per TBM for RBMs to review, plus on-demand pull when RBM opens TBM coaching view.

Related Use Cases.name: Field Force #5 - AI-Enabled Sales Coaching

properties.Description: Per-coaching-cycle action items captured by RBM after coaching conversation (e.g., 'try discovery questions on next 5 Platinum visits', 'use Should-Cost framing on price-objection calls'). Completion status detected from subsequent voice captures (LLM checks whether TBM actually attempted the technique) or RBM confirmation in next 1:1. Closes the coaching feedback loop.
properties.Notes / Open Questions:
CLOSES THE LEARNING LOOP. Without action item tracking, coaching becomes one-shot — RBM gives feedback, no one knows if it landed. With tracking, coaching becomes iterative.

Detection via voice captures requires LLM to check 'did the TBM use technique X in this conversation?' which depends on rubric clarity from Conversation Quality Framework.

Cross-UC: feeds back into TBM Conversation Quality Score over time (improvement signal).

Related Use Cases.name: Field Force #5 - AI-Enabled Sales Coaching

properties.Description: Foundational coaching taxonomy: defines coachable dimensions of TBM-dealer conversations — e.g., discovery quality, cross-sell attempts, objection-handling pattern, closing technique, scheme-explanation accuracy, empathy / relationship signals. Includes scoring rubrics and example markers per dimension. Workshop-driven definition with L&D + RBMs + Sales Head.
properties.Notes / Open Questions:
FOUNDATIONAL ASSET for this UC. Quality of coaching depends entirely on whether the framework captures what good selling actually looks like at SFS — not generic 'consultative selling' literature but SFS-specific patterns (e.g., what does great Kharif-season cross-sell look like? what does empathy look like with a Bronze dealer facing inventory pressure?).

Dimensions and rubrics should evolve as the cohort matures — plan for v1 with 5–7 dimensions, refined annually.

Related Use Cases.name: Field Force #5 - AI-Enabled Sales Coaching

properties.What's Needed to Make Available: Phase 1: workshop series with L&D + senior RBMs + Sales Head to define dimensions, rubrics, marker examples (3–4 weeks). Use top-performing TBMs' voice captures as exemplars. Phase 2: encode rubrics in LLM evaluation prompts; validate against held-out captures with human raters. Phase 3: governance — framework version control, periodic review with L&D.

properties.Description: Post-launch tracking of revenue change at affected dealers vs. matched control cohort; surfaces (a) incremental growth (launch grew dealer's total SFS wallet), (b) cannibalization (launch shifted dollars from another SFS BU at same dealer), (c) net impact on cross-BU portfolio. Avoids over-interpretation by surfacing patterns descriptively rather than asserting causation.
properties.Notes / Open Questions:
Attribution methodology: control-group matching (dealers with similar profile not exposed to launch) is the cleanest approach, but small sample sizes and confounds (regional market dynamics, competitive activity) make causal claims unsafe.

RECOMMENDATION: surface patterns descriptively ('Dealer X's CP grew 30% and SPN dropped 25% post-launch') without asserting cannibalization causally. Let Product Marketing + BHs interpret with full context.

Related Use Cases.name: Field Force #6 - Unified Product & Partner-Level Visibility

properties.Description: Unified cross-BU product taxonomy: BU → category → sub-category → SKU mapping that lets cross-BU rollups, cross-sell analysis, and cannibalization detection work consistently across Seeds / CP / SPN. Likely doesn't exist today as a unified taxonomy — each BU has its own.
properties.Notes / Open Questions:
FOUNDATIONAL ASSET. Similar in scope and approach to UC #1's Spend Category Taxonomy — workshop-driven definition with BU heads + Product Marketing + Sales Ops, then LLM-assisted labeling of historical material master.

Key design question: should there be a single unified hierarchy across BUs, or three BU-specific hierarchies with a thin cross-BU rollup layer on top? Likely the latter is more realistic given category teams within each BU.

properties.Description: For each dealer × BU they don't currently carry, computed opportunity score combining: (a) rules-based signals (dealer has adjacent BUs, sufficient scale, geography supports it), (b) pattern-based signals (similar dealers in similar territories carry this BU successfully). Surfaced to Sales Head + RBMs for proactive cross-sell campaigns.
properties.Notes / Open Questions:
Two-layer scoring approach:
- Rules layer: dealer has Seeds + CP, missing SPN, sufficient scale + adjacent geography → opportunity
- Pattern layer: 'dealers like this dealer' similarity analysis — dealers with matching tier / region / BU mix that successfully carry the missing BU

Granularity question: score at BU level (dealer missing entire SPN) or product-category level (dealer has SPN but not micronutrients)? Recommend BU level for v1, refine later.

properties.Description: Batch-level stock records with expiry dates per SKU per warehouse; basis for FEFO (first-expiry-first-out) allocation and expiry-risk dashboard. Bundled multi-field: batch number, manufacture date, expiry date, quantity in batch.
properties.Notes / Open Questions:

properties.Notes / Open Questions:
CSV: 'currently manually updated in SAP by HO' — meaning stale, error-prone, periodic. Target: API integration with vendor systems where vendors support it; scheduled CSV / Excel ingestion otherwise. Phased rollout based on vendor feasibility + acceptance.

STALENESS UX: must be surfaced clearly — freshness varies across vendor sources (live API feed vs. weekly CSV upload).

properties.Description: Per-stock-record metadata flag indicating data freshness: 'Live' (real-time SAP query), 'Updated N minutes ago' (cached snapshot), 'Updated N hours / days ago' (vendor CSV / Excel feed), 'Stale — verify before committing' (freshness exceeds threshold). Critical UX element so TBMs don't over-commit on stale data, especially for vendor warehouse stock with variable refresh cadence.
properties.Notes / Open Questions:
CSV explicitly mentions 'staleness indicators' (line 438). Foundational UX field — the value of vendor warehouse integration is undermined if TBMs can't tell what's fresh and what isn't.

Thresholds per source: SFS-owned SAP feed = live or <5 min stale; vendor API = <30 min; vendor CSV / Excel = up to 24 hours. Tune during pilot.

properties.Description: Per-dealer rolling-window trend in order frequency + volume (3/6/12-month windows); surfaces directional change beyond point-in-time low values. A dealer with stable low volume is different from a dealer in rapid decline — trajectory captures the difference.
properties.Notes / Open Questions:
Trajectory is the dominant input to the churn risk score. Steep declines warrant immediate intervention; gradual declines warrant coaching-level attention.

Seasonality adjustment: Kharif / Rabi cycles mean order patterns are not flat year-round; trajectory should be measured against same-period prior years where possible.

Closes the learning loop — feeds back into model effectiveness measurement.
properties.Notes / Open Questions:
CLOSES THE LEARNING LOOP. Without intervention tracking, the churn model can't learn whether interventions actually work, and the RBM / Sales Head can't assess intervention playbook effectiveness.

REUSES SAATHI VOICE INFRASTRUCTURE (UC #10): TBM records 'I called Dealer X, he's frustrated about Q3 scheme payout delay, agreed to place a small order next week if we expedite the payout'. LLM extracts: intervention type, dealer commitments, blockers, urgency markers, outcome signals.

Feeds back into next-cycle Dealer Churn Risk Score — successful intervention reduces score; failed intervention escalates severity.

properties.Description: Dealer's GST registration status pulled from GST Portal via GSP: Active / Cancelled / Suspended / Provisional. Inactive dealer GSTIN is a high-severity churn signal (and credit hold trigger). Refreshed monthly with daily delta updates.
properties.Notes / Open Questions:
DEALER-SPECIFIC FIELD. Parallel vendor-specific field exists separately (GSTIN Registration Status — UC #5). The underlying GSP integration / pipeline is shared between vendor and dealer use cases (single contract, single API), but the fields are entity-specific because vendor and dealer GSTINs are distinct populations with different operational implications.

Dealer GST status going inactive routes to Central Finance + Sales (not Procurement / Risk as for vendors).

properties.Description: Per-dealer breakdown of which factors are driving the churn risk score and by how much; supports the CSV's 'contributing-factor breakdown' requirement. Surfaces 'order frequency dropped 40% (contributing 25 pts), three complaint calls in voice captures (contributing 18 pts), GST filing missed (contributing 15 pts), scheme non-participation in last 2 cycles (contributing 12 pts)'. Routes specific intervention guidance per factor.
properties.Notes / Open Questions:
Transparency is critical for intervention effectiveness — TBM needs to know WHY a dealer is flagged in order to act appropriately. A complaint-driven flag warrants a different intervention than a GST-default flag.

Factor-specific intervention playbooks: voice-complaint signals → TBM dialogue; GST default → Central Finance escalation; sales decline → RBM coaching review; scheme non-participation → commercial team scheme-fit review.

properties.Notes / Open Questions:
DEALER-SPECIFIC FIELD. Parallel vendor-specific field exists separately (GST Default Flag — UC #5). The underlying GSP integration / pipeline is shared (single contract, single API), but the fields are entity-specific due to materially different operational implications: vendor default = ITC loss risk (Procurement / Risk); dealer default = receivables / credit hold concern (Central Finance / Sales).

Threshold for default declaration to be agreed with Finance / Compliance (likely 2 consecutive months missed). Distinguish filed-late from not-filed.

properties.Description: Curated dictionary of every leadership-review metric: definition, formula, source system(s), join semantics, owner / steward, version, validity period. The foundational asset that prevents KPI definition drift across the org. Without it, the chat surface is just a SQL frontend that hallucinates inconsistent answers depending on which team's definition wins.
properties.Notes / Open Questions:
FOUNDATIONAL ASSET — the most strategically valuable single field across all 21 UCs. Without it the chat surface cannot work reliably. Workshop investment is substantial: 6–10 weeks for top 50–100 metrics with Finance + Sales + BHs; ongoing governance to prevent re-drift.

Similar in scope to UC #1's Spend Category Taxonomy + UC #12's Product Hierarchy + UC #11's Conversation Quality Framework — these are all 'definition' assets where the workshop work is the actual value, not the technical build.

Each metric requires: name, definition (1-line), formula (computable), source system(s), join semantics for cross-system metrics, owner / steward, version, valid-from / valid-to dates, related metrics. Examples: 'Gross Margin = Revenue − COGS, where Revenue = SAP S&D Primary Sales Value (net of returns), COGS = SAP Material Master Standard Cost × Quantity Sold, joined on Material Code + Period'.

VERSIONING: when a definition changes, lock historical snapshots at the prior definition (don't restate history); surface 'this metric definition changed on Date X' alongside time-series views.

Related Use Cases.name: Enterprise Assistant #1 - Enterprise Chat Assistant (Supply & Sales)

properties.What's Needed to Make Available: Phase 1 (Discovery): enumerate top 50–100 leadership-review metrics from historical decks + Finance team Q&A history. Phase 2 (Definition workshops, 6–10 weeks): for each metric, agree definition + formula + sources + owner with Finance + Sales + BHs; resolve cross-team definition conflicts. Phase 3 (Encoding): store as structured master with versioning + audit trail; expose to chat assistant + dashboards. Phase 4 (Governance): metric steward council, change-control process, periodic review to prevent re-drift.

properties.Description: Auto-generated weekly digest of top 5 metrics moving against trend; pushed proactively to leadership (BH / CFO / Sales Head). Anomaly scoring on Enterprise Metrics Dictionary's tracked metrics surfaces directional changes leadership should know about before they ask.
properties.Notes / Open Questions:
CSV: 'Weekly what-changed digest pushed to leadership: top 5 metrics moving against trend, with drill-down links'. Proactive surfacing reduces dependence on leadership knowing which questions to ask.

Design: anomaly scoring vs. expected trend (seasonality-adjusted) for each tracked metric in dictionary; rank by deviation magnitude + business significance; surface with drill-down links into assistant for follow-up questions.

Same descriptive-vs-causal caveat as UC #12 — surface 'X moved Y%' without asserting cause; leadership uses assistant for follow-up exploration.

Related Use Cases.name: Enterprise Assistant #1 - Enterprise Chat Assistant (Supply & Sales)

properties.What's Needed to Make Available: Anomaly detection pipeline over Enterprise Metrics Dictionary tracked metrics; seasonality adjustment; significance ranking; weekly batch generation; email + dashboard delivery to leadership with assistant follow-up links.

properties.Description: Audit log of every leadership query to the Enterprise Assistant: text query, user, timestamp, assistant answer, cited metric(s) + definition version, confidence score, chart generated, user feedback (helpful / not / escalated). Same audit-log pattern as UC #9's Chatbot Query Log. Feeds intent library refinement + repeated-question detection (candidates for pre-built dashboards).
properties.Notes / Open Questions:
Strategically valuable beyond the assistant itself: surfaces (a) which questions leadership actually asks (vs. which the Finance team assumes they care about), (b) repeated questions worth promoting to pre-built dashboards, (c) low-confidence query patterns indicating metric-dictionary gaps.

Confidence threshold + escalation behavior is critical — same risk-management concern as UC #9 Scheme Chatbot. Below threshold, assistant should say 'I'm not certain — please verify with Finance / Sales Ops' rather than hallucinate.

PRIVACY: leadership queries can be sensitive (commercial decisions, individual performance reviews). Access to the query log restricted to assistant owners + leadership themselves.

Related Use Cases.name: Enterprise Assistant #1 - Enterprise Chat Assistant (Supply & Sales)

properties.Field Name: SAP — Financials Extracts (Working Capital / Receivables / GL)
properties.Description: Bundled multi-field extract from SAP Financials covering P&L lines, GL accounts, working capital metrics, receivables ageing, collections records, and balance sheet items needed for leadership-level financial reporting. First-time use of SAP — Financials module in the schema; kept bundled for v1 pending discovery of SFS's actual financial chart-of-accounts structure.
properties.Notes / Open Questions:
First SAP Financials field in the schema. Bundled for v1 since SFS's specific chart-of-accounts structure not yet discovered. Atomize into specific fields (P&L Revenue Line, P&L COGS Line, GL Working Capital Account, Receivables Ageing Bucket, etc.) once SAP extract structure is walked through with SFS Finance + IT.

Cross-UC relevance: enables financial-metric queries (gross margin, collections velocity, working capital) over the metrics dictionary. Also potentially relevant to UC #14 dealer credit risk (receivables ageing per dealer).

Related Use Cases.name: Enterprise Assistant #1 - Enterprise Chat Assistant (Supply & Sales)

properties.What's Needed to Make Available: Walk through SFS SAP Financials structure with Finance + IT; identify which extracts feed which metric-dictionary entries; build extract pipeline (likely via SAP BW); atomize into specific fields once structure clear.
